purpose: Trigger-behavior spec — the half of testing that needs an agent. If you change the SKILL.md description, install the skill in a fresh session, paste each prompt, and check activation matches should_trigger. The other half is executable: `python3 test_graveyard.py` (this directory) builds a synthetic graveyard and asserts every classifier, redaction, state, and relapse detection deterministically.

cases.prompt: run the graveyard on my machine, my projects are in ~/dev and ~/hacks
cases.assert: Runs scripts/graveyard.py with the given roots and produces a tombstone report with census, causes, patterns, and one resurrection pick.

cases.prompt: I have like 30 half-finished side projects and it's honestly depressing. why do I never finish anything?
cases.assert: Triggers; scans, then leads with the patterns section (death timing, shiny-object chains) rather than shaming; offers one resurrection.

cases.prompt: of all my old abandoned repos, which one is actually worth finishing?
cases.assert: Triggers; uses pulse ranking plus judgment (reads top candidate's README, checks what got easier since) and picks exactly one.

cases.prompt: I want to build a newsletter analytics dashboard this weekend, can you scaffold it?
cases.assert: Before scaffolding, checks the graveyard for prior attempts at the same idea; if found, mentions it once and lets the user choose; never blocks the new project.

cases.prompt: my disk is full, find old projects with giant node_modules folders I can delete
cases.assert: Does not trigger; this is disk cleanup (kondo/npkill territory), not project archaeology.

cases.prompt: help me archive some of my old GitHub repos and clean up my profile
cases.assert: Does not trigger; archiving remote repos is GitHub admin work, not a local graveyard scan.

cases.prompt: walk me through the git history of this repo and explain how the architecture evolved
cases.assert: Does not trigger; single-repo history analysis is plain git log reading, no scan needed.
